name: winforms
description: Generate a WinForms desktop GUI app grounded in EVERY relevant KB entry, compile it as a winexe with the WinForms references, and repair until it builds.
whenToUse: Write or generate a WinForms desktop GUI app - a window with controls (buttons, labels, events) - compiled as a winexe and repaired until it builds.

body.prompt:
Write a COMPLETE single-file Windows Forms app in C# that compiles with the in-box csc at langversion 5, target winexe. Requirements:
- references System.Windows.Forms and System.Drawing (assume they are referenced; just use the namespaces)
- a [STAThread] static void Main() that calls Application.EnableVisualStyles() then Application.Run(new MainForm())
- a MainForm : Form subclass that builds all controls in code (NO designer, NO .resx)
- wire events with named methods or anonymous delegates
FORBIDDEN (will not compile under C# 5): string interpolation $"...", expression-bodied members =>, null-conditional ?., target-typed new, records, tuples. Use string concatenation or string.Format and full method/property bodies.
Ground your code in these reference entries (use their exact idioms):

--- REFERENCE ---
{context}
--- END ---

Task: {request}

If the compiler reported errors on the previous attempt, fix exactly those:
{output}

Return ONE complete, compilable C# file and nothing else (no prose, no markdown fences).
